source_intake_record.raw_question_text: A cart of mass $m$ moves with speed $v$ as it approaches a cart of mass $3m$ that is initially at rest. The spring is compressed during the head-on collision. What is the speed of the cart with mass $3m$ at the instant of maximum spring compression assuming conservation of energy?

candidate_problem_record.subject: 物理
candidate_problem_record.raw_question_text: A cart of mass $m$ moves with speed $v$ as it approaches a cart of mass $3m$ that is initially at rest. The spring is compressed during the head-on collision. What is the speed of the cart with mass $3m$ at the instant of maximum spring compression assuming conservation of energy?
candidate_problem_record.multi_solution_policy_rationale: 该数据集保留多解潜力评估，但不默认强推多解 agent。

extraction_notes: image field contains a PIL image representation rather than a file path or URL
metadata.reasoning_chain: When the spring is at maximum compression, the two carts are nearest each other and at that instant move with a velocity $v'$, say. Conservation of momentum gives $m v=(m+3m)v'$, i.e. $v' = \frac{v}{4}$. Thus the heavier cart has speed $\frac{v}{4}$ at that instant.

problem_main_record.normalized_question_text: A cart of mass $m$ moves with speed $v$ as it approaches a cart of mass $3m$ that is initially at rest. The spring is compressed during the head-on collision. What is the speed of the cart with mass $3m$ at the instant of maximum spring compression assuming conservation of energy?
problem_main_record.reasoning_chain: When the spring is at maximum compression, the two carts are nearest each other and at that instant move with a velocity $v'$, say. Conservation of momentum gives $m v=(m+3m)v'$, i.e. $v' = \frac{v}{4}$. Thus the heavier cart has speed $\frac{v}{4}$ at that instant.
